source_metadata.title_raw: Scott W. Lay - A-18-013 - February 7, 2018 - Grass Valley

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALI FORNI A 
\ FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 11 1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
1 (916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
J 
February 7, 2018 
Scott W. Lay 
Nevada County Superintendent of Schools 
380 Crown Point Circle 
Grass Valley, CA 95945 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-013 
Dear Mr. Lay: 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the 
provisions of the Act as the Commission does not provide advice on bodies of law outside of the 
Act, including other laws limiting the use of public funds. Also, note that we are not a finder of fact 
when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes 
your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions 
should change, you should contact us for additional advice. 
QUESTION 
Under the mass mailings provisions of the Act, may your office pay for an insert in a local 
newspaper that includes an annual report containing your name, title, and photograph? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. Your office may pay for an annual report to be included in the local newspaper and 
distributed to its subscribers as the Act's mass mailings provisions do not prohibit you from paying 
for the insert. Additionally, you may make copies of the annual report available at your office for 
pick-up by the general public. 
FACTS 
You are the Nevada County Superintendent of Schools. You are preparing a 24-page annual 
report, which provides information on the state of the schools and education in Nevada County (the 
"Report"). The Report will include information and photographs of students and staff in educational 
programs from the County, as well as your name, title, and photograph. You intend to print 20,000 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 181 JO through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless othenvise indicated. 


File No. A-18-013 
Page No. 2 
copies of the Report. The cost to design, produce, print, and distribute the Report will amount to 
approximately $2,500, of which, approximately 75-percent of those costs will be paid for by public 
funds from your office. The remaining 25-percent will be paid for by a private health-related grant. 
Of the 20,000 copies of the Report, 19,950 copies will be included as an insert in the Union, a local 
newspaper of general circulation, and distributed one copy per subscriber. You plan on placing the 
remaining 50 copies on the front counter of your office for pick up by the general public. Your 
office will not distribute any other copies. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 89001 establishes that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public 
expense." Regulation 18901 ( a) prohibits a mailing, for purposes of Section 89001, if all of the 
following criteria are met: 
"{l) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her 
residence, place of employment or business, or post office box ... 
"(2) The item sent either: 
"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or 
sends the mailing, or 
"(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an elected 
officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is 
prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the 
elected officer. 
"(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public money; or 
"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 are paid 
with public moneys, and the design, production, or printing is done with the intent of 
sending the item other than as permitted by this regulation. 
"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a single 
calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and 
any item described in subdivision (b)." 
From your facts, the pertinent question is whether including the Report as an insert in the 
Union meets the definition of "items sent in response to an unsolicited request," and is thereby 
excluded from the 200-item limit under Regulation 1890l(a)(4). Regulation 1890l(c)(4)(E) defines 
an ''unsolicited request" to include, "a request for a subscription to a newspaper or other periodical 
published by non-elected officers." In this case, you have indicated that the copies will be 
distributed one copy per subscriber. Therefore, these copies of the Report are excluded from the 
mass mailings prohibition under Regulation 1890l(a)(4) and 18901(c)(4)(E). 
With regard to the 50 copies of the Report you plan to make available for the public at your 
office, we have previously advised that an item is not "sent" and does not count against the 200-


File No. A-18-013 
Page No. 3 
item per month limit if the item is made available for members of the public to talce. (Pittard 
Advice Letter, No. A-16-107.) Therefore, the Act does not prohibit your office from placing copies 
of the Report on your front counter for pick-up by the general public. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
By: 
Counsel, Legal Division 
SKP:jgl
